hero.embedded_thesis: At A$3.84, the current price embeds a scenario between the base case and a partial recovery - the market is pricing the Virginia facility milestone as real but not yet revenue-generating, a DFS completion in Q2 2026 with broadly positive but not transformative economics, and a cash runway sufficient to avoid near-term dilution. The Forward P/E of -169.1x and negative EV/EBITDA of -23.6x confirm the market is valuing IperionX entirely on option value and strategic positioning rather than current earnings. The +33.3% single-day move to A$3.84 - driven by director buying, the Virginia campus going live, and short covering - suggests the market is partially unwinding the post-H1 de-rating, but the stock remains approximately 58% below its 52-week high of A$9.10, implying the market still assigns meaningful probability to a prolonged pre-revenue grind or further negative catalysts.
hero.skew_description: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with N2 (base case neutral grind, 38%) carrying the highest weight, appropriate for a development-stage company with a live operational facility but zero commercial revenue. N3 (bear case, 24%) has been reduced from 33% as the Virginia milestone and director buying partially offset the short-seller and governance risks. N1 (recovery, 28%) has been upgraded to reflect the operational progress and insider conviction signals. The overall skew is modestly positive but fragile, with the DFS release in Q2 2026 as the next major binary catalyst.

next_decision_point.metric: DFS NPV relative to current market cap of approximately A$1.3 billion, IRR, capex estimate, and timeline to first commercial production
next_decision_point.thresholds: Positive shift: DFS delivers NPV materially above A$1.3B with IRR above 20% and confirms first commercial production within 12 months - shifts weight decisively toward N1 and supports A$6-A$8 range. Negative shift: DFS NPV below current market cap, IRR below 15%, or timeline to production pushed beyond 24 months - re-rates toward N3 and A$2.00-A$2.50 range. Neutral: DFS in line with expectations - stock consolidates in A$3.50-A$5.00 range.

heroCompanyDescription: IperionX Limited (ASX: IPX) is an Australian-listed, US-focused critical minerals and advanced materials company developing a domestic titanium supply chain in the United States. The company uses patented metal technologies to produce performance titanium alloys from titanium minerals or scrap titanium at lower energy, cost, and carbon emissions than conventional processes - a differentiated position in the context of the US push to onshore critical mineral supply chains.

skew.rationale: The probability distribution is skewed bearish at initiation, with N3 (downside) and N2 (neutral grind) together accounting for 70% of the probability mass. The accounting correction, accelerating cash burn, Spruce Point overhang, and heavy institutional selling volume in the week of March 16-19, 2026 represent a cluster of negative signals that outweigh the structural onshoring tailwind in the near term. The bull case (N1 at 20%) requires multiple simultaneous positive outcomes that are not yet in evidence.

verdict.text: At A$3.84, IperionX is priced for a partial recovery from its post-H1 de-rating, with the market assigning meaningful value to the Virginia facility milestone and insider buying signal. The stock remains approximately 58% below its 52-week high of A$9.10 and approximately 55% below the Wall Street consensus target of US$8.58, implying the market is not yet fully pricing the bull case. The +33.3% single-day rally is notable but occurred against a backdrop of broad commodity weakness and mining equity selloffs, suggesting it is driven by company-specific factors - director buying, short covering, and the Virginia campus confirmation - rather than macro tailwinds. The fundamental reality has not changed: IperionX is pre-revenue, burning approximately US$6M per month, and the path to commercial scale runs through a DFS that has not yet been released. The next major binary is the Q2 2026 DFS - if it delivers strong economics, the stock has a credible path toward A$6-A$8; if it disappoints or is delayed, today's rally is likely to retrace toward A$2.50-A$3.00.

featuredRationale: The four hypotheses sum to 100%, with N2 (base case neutral grind, 38%) carrying the highest weight as is appropriate for initial coverage of a development-stage company. N3 (bear case, 32%) carries unusually high weight for a base-case initiation, reflecting the cluster of negative near-term signals. N1 (bull case, 20%) and N4 (tail catalyst, 10%) together represent 30% upside probability, anchored by the genuine strategic value of the onshoring thesis and government funding validation.

identity.overview: IperionX Limited (ASX: IPX) — auto-added to coverage. Full company overview pending analyst research.

hypotheses.title: N1: Growth/Recovery
hypotheses.description: IperionX completes its Definitive Feasibility Study (DFS) in Q2 2026 with strong economics, secures additional US government offtake or grant commitments, and begins generating first commercial revenue from its Virginia facility within 12 months. The Virginia campus is now online, director buying signals insider conviction, and the Wall Street consensus target of US$8.58 implies substantial upside from the current A$3.84.
supporting: US$60M+ in secured government funding provides credible institutional validation of the onshoring thesis
Q2 2026 DFS completion is a near-term hard catalyst that could reset the narrative
7x capacity expansion at Virginia facility signals management confidence in commercial scale-up
Structural tailwind from US push to onshore titanium manufacturing and reduce China supply chain dependency
Cash position of US$65.85M provides runway to reach first commercial sales without immediate dilution
contradicting: Company remains pre-revenue with zero commercial sales despite years of development, raising timeline credibility questions
Net loss widened to US$34.77M in H1 2026 from US$16.24M a year earlier, accelerating cash burn
Spruce Point hinting at a second short-seller attack could suppress re-rating even if fundamentals improve
Accounting correction to H1 results undermines confidence in financial governance at a critical juncture
Broader commodity price weakness and World Bank six-year-low forecast create a difficult macro backdrop

hypotheses.title: N2: Base Case
hypotheses.description: IperionX continues its pre-revenue development trajectory, with the DFS expected in Q2 2026 and the Virginia facility 7x capacity expansion progressing on schedule, but first commercial revenue remains 12-18 months away. The stock stabilises in the A$3.50-A$5.00 range as the market digests the Virginia campus milestone and awaits DFS economics. Cash of US$65.85M and US$60M+ in government funding provide adequate runway.
supporting: US$65.85M cash and US$60M+ government funding provide a credible 12-18 month operational runway
DFS completion in Q2 2026 is a defined near-term milestone that could provide incremental positive newsflow
Dual listing on ASX and Nasdaq broadens the investor base and provides access to US capital markets
Patented metal technologies for lower-energy, lower-cost titanium production represent a genuine differentiated asset
contradicting: Accounting correction and widening losses have damaged near-term sentiment and invited short-seller scrutiny
Zero commercial sales after years of development raises questions about the pace of commercialisation
Heavy selling volume (144% above 90-day average) on March 19 suggests institutional distribution, not just retail panic
World Bank commodity price forecast at six-year lows creates a difficult environment for mining/materials re-ratings

hypotheses.title: N3: Risk/Downside
hypotheses.description: The accounting correction and widening losses remain on record, and Spruce Point's hinted second short-seller attack has not been formally withdrawn. If a second report surfaces with substantive new allegations, or if the DFS disappoints, the stock could re-test the A$2.00-A$2.50 range. The market's willingness to rally +33.3% in a single session on no new fundamental news also raises the risk of a sharp reversal.
supporting: Accounting correction issued just two days after initial H1 filing raises serious internal controls concerns
Spruce Point Capital hinting at a second short-seller report is a material overhang on the stock
Net loss nearly doubled year-on-year to US$34.77M with zero commercial revenue to offset burn
Heavy volume selling (144% above average) on March 19 suggests informed sellers, not just noise
Stock already down 42% from March high of A$6.93 to current A$3.60, with momentum firmly negative
Section 708A notice in February 2026 suggests recent capital raising, implying potential ongoing dilution risk
contradicting: US$65.85M cash position and US$60M+ government grants reduce near-term insolvency risk
Company denied undisclosed negative news in ASX price query response, suggesting no hidden catastrophic event
US government strategic interest in titanium onshoring provides a political backstop to funding continuity
DFS completion in Q2 2026 could provide a positive reset if economics are compelling

hypotheses.title: N4: Disruption/Catalyst
hypotheses.description: A major US defence contractor, aerospace prime, or strategic acquirer recognises IperionX's patented low-cost titanium technology and Virginia facility as a critical national security asset and moves to acquire or enter a transformative offtake agreement. Alternatively, a significant expansion of US government funding or a formal defence procurement contract could re-rate the stock toward A$10+. This remains a tail scenario with a 10% probability.
supporting: US government has already committed US$60M+ in grants, demonstrating strategic interest at the highest level
Patented technology for lower-energy, lower-cost titanium from scrap or minerals is strategically valuable to defence supply chains
US-China trade tensions and critical mineral supply chain concerns are intensifying, raising the strategic premium on domestic titanium capacity
Dual Nasdaq/ASX listing increases visibility to US strategic and institutional investors
contradicting: Company is pre-revenue with no demonstrated commercial-scale production, making acquisition valuation highly uncertain
Accounting correction and short-seller scrutiny would complicate any due diligence process
No current public signals of M&A interest or strategic partnership discussions
Widening losses and governance questions reduce attractiveness as an acquisition target at current development stage

narrative.theNarrative: [Updated 02-Apr-26 09:04 AEST] IperionX's narrative has undergone a sharp intraday reversal on April 1, 2026, with the stock surging +33.3% to A$3.84 - partially recovering from the 53% peak-to-trough collapse that followed the H1 2026 results and accounting correction in mid-March. The catalyst for today's rally appears to be a combination of factors: the Virginia titanium manufacturing campus is now confirmed online, with approximately 290 metric tons of titanium scrap transferred free of charge to feed initial operations, and multiple directors made significant on-market share purchases on March 29, including CEO Anastasios Arima (467,000 shares, approximately US$1.33 million) and Todd Hannigan (225,225 shares, approximately US$498,000). The broader macro backdrop remains challenging - copper fell -4.7% and gold collapsed -9.6% today, with the World Bank projecting commodity prices at six-year lows in 2026 - yet IperionX's rally in the face of this sector-wide selloff reinforces the thesis that its critical minerals and onshoring positioning insulates it from traditional commodity cycles. The company remains pre-revenue with zero commercial sales, and the H1 2026 net loss of US$34.77 million (more than double the prior year) reflects heavy infrastructure reinvestment rather than operational failure. The Wall Street consensus target of US$8.58 implies substantial upside from current levels, but the path to that target runs through the Q2 2026 DFS release and the conversion of the Virginia facility from prototype to commercial production. The Spruce Point second-report threat remains an unresolved overhang, and the accounting correction on March 13 has not been forgotten by the market.

priceImplication.label: Embedded Assumptions at A$3.84
priceImplication.content:
At A$3.84, the current price appears to embed:

- A commercialisation timeline of 12-18 months to first commercial revenue, modestly shorter than the 18-24 months the market was pricing at the post-correction low near A$2.50-A$3.00
- The Virginia facility milestone as real and de-risking, but not yet revenue-generating - the market is paying for optionality on conversion from prototype to commercial scale
- A non-trivial probability (estimated 25-35%) of a dilutive capital raise before first revenue, given the H1 2026 burn rate of approximately US$34.77M over six months against a cash position of US$65.85M
- Ongoing but reduced short-seller overhang from Spruce Point's hinted second report, partially offset by the director buying signal
- A DFS in Q2 2026 that confirms broadly positive economics, without necessarily delivering a transformative NPV surprise
- Continued US government support for domestic titanium supply chains, with the US$60M+ in grant funding treated as largely locked in

narrative.evidenceCheck: The evidence base has strengthened materially on the operational side but remains thin on commercial validation. The Virginia campus going live and the 290-tonne titanium scrap transfer are concrete, verifiable milestones that reduce execution risk. Director purchases totalling approximately US$1.83 million across multiple insiders on March 29 represent a meaningful insider conviction signal - these are not token purchases. The Wall Street consensus target of US$8.58 provides an external anchor for the bull case. However, the company still has zero commercial revenue, the H1 2026 net loss of US$34.77 million is a verified fact, and the accounting correction on March 13 remains on record as a governance blemish. The Spruce Point second-report threat is unverified but not withdrawn. The macro backdrop - copper -4.7%, gold -9.6%, World Bank projecting six-year commodity price lows - is a genuine headwind for mining equity sentiment broadly, even if IperionX's specific demand drivers are differentiated. On balance, the evidence supports a modest upward revision to N1 and a reduction in N3, but does not yet justify a wholesale shift to the bull case.
narrative.narrativeStability: The dominant narrative - development-stage onshoring bet recovering from a governance shock, now anchored by an operational milestone - is more stable than it was two weeks ago but still highly event-dependent. The March sell-off has been partially absorbed, the accounting correction is in the past, and the Virginia campus milestone provides a new positive anchor. The narrative now rests on two near-term tests: first, whether the Q2 2026 DFS delivers economics that justify the current market cap of approximately A$1.3 billion; and second, whether Spruce Point publishes a second report and, if so, whether IperionX can rebut it credibly. A DFS with NPV materially above the current market cap and IRR above 20% would shift weight decisively toward N1/N4. A Spruce Point second report with substantive new allegations - particularly around grant eligibility, related-party transactions, or technology claims - could reverse today's rally entirely. The +33.3% single-day move also introduces its own fragility: a rally of this magnitude on no new fundamental news (no offtake agreement, no DFS, no revenue) is vulnerable to profit-taking and could partially retrace.

evidence.intro: Evidence is assessed across ten epistemic domains, ranked by reliability. Each domain carries a distinct epistemic quality – from verified regulatory filings to unverified media commentary – ensuring the analyst understands what the evidence is before assessing what it means.

cards.title: 1. Corporate Communications
cards.finding: IperionX Limited responded to an ASX price query on March 16, 2026, denying any undisclosed news behind a sharp share price fall. The company highlighted its US grant funding and ongoing strategic developments. Following this, the stock has seen a significant rally, with the current price at A$3.84, up +33.3% today, and news headlines like 'IperionX (ASX:IPX) Stock Soars: Key Reasons Behind the Rally' (kalkine.com.au) indicating a shift in market perception.
cards.tension: The market's initial lack of reassurance following the company's denial has been challenged by the substantial +33.3% rally today, suggesting a renewed focus on the company's underlying growth story and strategic developments, potentially indicating a reversal of negative sentiment.
cards.source: ASX: Response to ASX Price Query (2026-03-16); TipRanks (2026-03-16); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-19)

cards.title: 2. Regulatory Filings & Financial Statements
cards.finding: IperionX reported Half Year Accounts on March 11, 2026, which were subsequently corrected on March 13, 2026, as confirmed by ASX announcements. The corrected accounts for H1 2026 (to December 31, 2025) showed a net loss widening to US$34.77 million, up from US$16.24 million a year earlier, with basic and diluted loss per share at US$0.10. News headlines on simplywall.st and stocksdownunder.com reiterated these sharply wider losses.
cards.tension: The significant widening of losses is attributed to heavy reinvestment in infrastructure and manufacturing, indicating a long-term growth strategy. However, the need for a correction to the accounts, highlighted by media as a 'typo blunder,' continues to raise concerns about financial reporting accuracy and has contributed to market negativity.
cards.source: ASX: Half Year Accounts (2026-03-11); ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13); simplywall.st (2026-03-16); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16)

cards.title: 3. Broker Research
cards.finding: Following the H1 2026 results and accounting correction, short sellers reportedly 'circled' IperionX, with Capitalbrief.com reporting Spruce Point hinting at a second attack. However, the stock has experienced a significant rally of +33.3% today, and analyst consensus shows a Wall Street target of US$8.5844, significantly above the current price, suggesting a strong long-term positive outlook from some analytical firms.
cards.tension: While short interest indicates a bearish view, the company's long-term narrative, aligning with the US push to onshore titanium manufacturing, continues to be a counter-argument for some analysts. The recent stock rally and high Wall Street target suggest a potential shift in sentiment or a strong counter-narrative against short sellers, despite intensified short-seller activity.

cards.title: 4. Competitor Disclosures
cards.finding: IperionX is described as an American producer of titanium alloys, titanium products, and critical minerals. However, no specific disclosures or performance data from direct competitors in the titanium or critical minerals sector were provided in the available inputs.
cards.tension: Without direct competitor disclosures, it is challenging to benchmark IperionX's operational performance, market share, or strategic positioning against its peers, limiting a comprehensive competitive analysis.

cards.title: 5. Economic Data
cards.finding: Global commodity prices are projected to hit six-year lows in March 2026, according to the World Bank. Today's data shows copper and gold prices declining significantly by -4.7% and -9.6% respectively. However, critical minerals are identified as a 'new commodity supercycle' driven by China's economic transformation, and headlines note supply disruptions for other industrial metals like aluminum due to geopolitical events.
cards.tension: While general commodity weakness, reinforced by today's declines in copper and gold, presents a headwind, IperionX's significant stock rally (+33.3%) today, despite these broader market trends, strengthens the argument that its focus on titanium and critical minerals, particularly within the context of the US push for onshoring, may insulate it from traditional commodity price declines and benefit from specific demand drivers.
cards.source: World Bank (investor.wedbush.com); Macro & Commodity Context; discoveryalert.com.au

cards.title: 6. Alternative Data
cards.finding: IperionX is undertaking 'heavy reinvestment in infrastructure, manufacturing' and plans a '7x capacity expansion at Virginia facility'. Recent news confirms that the Virginia titanium manufacturing campus is now online, with government funding largely locked in, and a free transfer of about 290 metric tons of titanium scrap to feed current operations. Despite these operational developments, the company remains 'pre-revenue with zero commercial sales' as of its H1 2026 results.
cards.tension: Significant capital expenditure and the operationalization of the Virginia facility indicate concrete progress towards future production and revenue generation, partially addressing the tension about the timeline for monetizing these investments. However, the continued absence of commercial sales, highlighted by the market's negative reaction to widening losses, still raises questions about the path to profitability.

cards.title: 7. Academic Research
cards.finding: While no specific academic research papers were provided, the company's strategy aligns with the broader 'US push to onshore titanium manufacturing and processing'. This trend is often supported by geopolitical and economic studies on supply chain resilience and critical mineral security.
cards.tension: The general strategic importance of titanium onshoring is a positive structural trend, but without specific peer-reviewed research on IperionX's technology or market, the direct impact and competitive advantage remain unquantified.

cards.title: 8. Media & Social
cards.finding: Media coverage was overwhelmingly negative following the H1 2026 results and accounting correction, with headlines such as 'Why IperionX (ASX:IPX) Is Down 23.9%'. However, the current day's significant price increase of +33.3% has led to positive media coverage, with headlines like 'IperionX (ASX:IPX) Stock Soars: Key Reasons Behind the Rally' (kalkine.com.au), indicating a material shift in sentiment.
cards.tension: The company denied any undisclosed news behind the earlier share slump, and the recent substantial rally suggests that the market reaction might have been an overcorrection. The shift to positive media coverage indicates a potential turning point in investor sentiment, moving beyond the previous negativity.

cards.title: 9. Leadership & Governance
cards.finding: IperionX issued a 'Correction to Half Year Accounts' on March 13, 2026, just two days after the initial release, indicating an error in financial reporting. However, recent ASX announcements (March 29, 2026) detail multiple 'Change of Director's Interest Notice' filings, with news reporting significant share acquisitions by directors, including Anastasios Arima (467,000 shares) and Todd Hannigan (225,225 shares).
cards.tension: While the initial blunder in a key financial report raised questions about internal controls, the subsequent significant share acquisitions by multiple directors demonstrate strong insider confidence. This insider buying could partially mitigate concerns about financial reporting accuracy and the robustness of the company's governance processes.
cards.source: ASX: Half Year Accounts (2026-03-11); ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13)

cards.title: 10. Ownership & Capital Flows
cards.finding: IperionX issued a 'Notice under Section 708A' on February 23, 2026, typically associated with capital raising. The company maintains a cash position of US$65.85 million and has secured over US$60 million in government funding. While previous trading saw shares fall on 'heavy volume', recent ASX announcements (March 29, 2026) and news reports detail significant share acquisitions by directors, including Anastasios Arima (467,000 shares valued at US$1.33 million) and Todd Hannigan (225,225 shares valued at US$498,226). The stock has also rallied +33.3% today.
cards.tension: The substantial cash reserves and government funding provide a financial runway for operations and expansion. The recent significant director share acquisitions represent material positive capital inflows from insiders, partially offsetting previous concerns about heavy selling volume and potential dilution from the Section 708A notice. The strong price rally also indicates a positive shift in overall capital flows.
cards.source: ASX: Notice under Section 708A (2026-02-23); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16 - earnings snippet); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-19)

evidence.alignmentSummary: Financial data consistently points to significant cash burn and widening losses, aligning with the company's pre-revenue status and ongoing investment in the Virginia facility. While operational progress is noted, the lack of independent verification and pending DFS results create uncertainty. Market sentiment appears volatile, with both rallies and sharp declines, reflecting the speculative nature of the investment and the unresolved short-seller overhang.

discriminators.intro: The following data points carry high diagnosticity – they can meaningfully shift probability between competing hypotheses.

rows.evidence: Q2 2026 DFS economics and timeline to first commercial production
rows.currentReading: DFS pending; expected Q2 2026. No preview data available. Company pre-revenue as of H1 2026.

rows.evidence: Spruce Point Capital second short-seller report publication and content
rows.currentReading: Hinted but not yet published as of March 20, 2026. Represents a live and unresolved overhang.

rows.evidence: Cash burn trajectory and capital raise requirement before first revenue
rows.currentReading: US$65.85M cash; H1 2026 burn approximately US$34.77M (though includes non-cash items). Runway estimated at 12-18 months at current rate.

discriminators.nonDiscriminating:
• Dual ASX/Nasdaq listing: Provides capital market access and visibility but does not discriminate between bull and bear cases -- the listing structure is neutral to the commercialisation outcome.
  • Patented technology claims: The existence of patents is verifiable but their commercial value is unproven at scale; patents alone do not discriminate between N1 and N3.
  • General commodity price weakness: The World Bank six-year-low forecast affects all materials companies; it is a headwind but does not specifically discriminate between IperionX's competing hypotheses, which are driven by company-specific execution risk.
  • Macro US onshoring policy: The structural tailwind from US critical minerals policy is present in all four scenarios; it is a background condition, not a discriminator between them.
  • 52-week high of A$9.10: Historical price levels reflect prior sentiment, not current fundamental value; the distance from the high is a sentiment indicator but does not discriminate between the four hypotheses.

tripwires.intro: The following catalysts could force a material revision to hypothesis weightings. Each carries conditional outcomes that map to specific narrative shifts.

conditions.if: If DFS delivers NPV materially above current market cap with IRR above 20% and confirms first commercial production within 12 months
conditions.then: Then N1 probability rises sharply; stock likely re-rates toward A$6.00-A$7.00 range as commercialisation timeline is validated

conditions.if: If DFS is delayed beyond Q2 2026, or economics are below market expectations, or release is accompanied by a dilutive capital raise
conditions.then: Then N3 probability rises; stock likely tests 52-week low of A$2.17 and Spruce Point attack becomes more credible

conditions.if: If Spruce Point publishes a second short-seller report and the company provides a credible, detailed rebuttal with independent verification
conditions.then: Then short overhang partially clears; stock stabilises and base case N2 is reinforced

conditions.if: If Spruce Point publishes a second short-seller report containing new material allegations about technology, financials, or governance that the company cannot rebut
conditions.then: Then N3 becomes dominant; stock likely falls 20-30% from current levels and institutional selling accelerates

cards.name: Full Year Accounts FY2026

conditions.if: If FY2026 full-year accounts are released cleanly with no corrections, show controlled cash burn, and include first commercial revenue or a signed offtake agreement
conditions.then: Then governance discount partially reverses; N2 base case is reinforced and N1 probability increases

conditions.if: If FY2026 accounts require further corrections, show cash burn accelerating beyond US$70M annualised, or reveal a capital raise requirement
conditions.then: Then N3 bear case is confirmed; stock likely breaks below A$2.17 52-week low and funding risk becomes acute

cards.source: ASX: Correction to Half Year Accounts (2026-03-13); kalkine.com.au (2026-03-16)

cards.name: Virginia Facility First Commercial Sale

conditions.if: If IperionX announces its first commercial titanium sale or signed offtake agreement from the Virginia facility before or at the May 2026 quarterly update
conditions.then: Then the pre-revenue overhang is broken, the commercialisation timeline compresses materially, and the stock has a credible path toward A$6-A$8 - shifts weight from N2 to N1 and reduces N3 probability significantly

conditions.if: If the May 2026 quarterly update confirms continued zero commercial revenue with no signed offtake agreements and cash burn accelerating above US$7M per month
conditions.then: Then dilution risk increases materially, the Virginia milestone is reframed as a prototype rather than a commercial asset, and the stock risks retracing toward A$2.50-A$3.00 - shifts weight from N1/N2 toward N3

cards.source: Stocksdownunder.com analysis: 'IperionX valuation now hinges on Virginia converting prototypes into commercial sales'; ASX quarterly activity report cycle

coverageRows.confidence: Medium -- company denials are motivated communications; limited forward guidance provided
Medium -- accounts verified but correction raises internal controls questions; no auditor commentary available
Medium -- Wall Street target provides some consensus, but formal broker reports and detailed rationale are still absent.
Low -- no benchmarking possible against titanium peers; competitive position unquantified

coverageRows.freshness: Feb 28, 2026 (RBA Yield Curve) and March 2026 (World Bank commodity outlook)
coverageRows.confidence: Medium -- macro context is clear (commodity headwinds) but titanium-specific price data not available
Low -- no independent verification of Virginia facility progress, capacity utilisation, or production metrics.

trend.structure: Price in downtrend relative to 50-day and 200-day moving averages.

footer.disclaimer: This report does not constitute personal financial advice. Continuum Intelligence synthesises cross-domain evidence using the Analysis of Competing Hypotheses (ACH) methodology. All factual claims are sourced from ASX filings, company disclosures, broker consensus data, and publicly available information.

priceDrivers.primary_driver: A recovery from deeply oversold post-earnings conditions, most likely catalysed or confirmed by director interest change filings on 29 March, drove IPX from its A$3.21 trough to A$3.84, with the precise trigger contingent on whether those filings represent open-market insider purchases.

primary: Director interest change filings (29 March) as a potential insider buying signal, conditional on transaction type being open-market purchases rather than routine equity plan events
Post-earnings capitulation reversal and technical mean reversion after a 46% drawdown from the 15 March H1 FY2026 earnings crash
secondary: Broad ASX200 and industrials/materials sector recovery providing a macro tailwind of approximately 3.7% over the same window
Short covering as post-earnings sellers closed positions into the bounce
amplifiers: Lagging retail media coverage from Kalkine and Stocks Down Under amplifying awareness of the recovery after it had already begun
Wide gap between analyst consensus target (A$8.56) and current price, reinforcing a mean-reversion narrative for re-engaging buyers
background: US$65.85M cash and US$60M+ in secured US government funding maintaining solvency despite widening operating losses
Virginia titanium manufacturing campus confirmed operational, preserving longer-term strategic credibility
Stock sitting 58% below its October 2025 peak of A$9.10, creating a deeply discounted entry point for risk-tolerant buyers
rejected: Macro or sector sympathy as the primary cause: IPX outperformed the ASX200 by 14 percentage points over five days, confirming the move is predominantly stock-specific
New positive company announcement or milestone: no market-sensitive ASX filing was lodged in the recovery window
Retail social media coordination: HotCopper, Reddit and X data are all absent; no evidence of a retail-driven squeeze
Broker upgrade or analyst re-rating: no broker notes were identified in the data, making this unconfirmed; it cannot be assigned primary status without evidence

report.title: IPX: Post-Earnings Bounce Accelerates on Director Filings, but Trigger Remains Unconfirmed
report.executive_summary: IperionX shares recovered from a post-earnings trough of A$3.21 (22 March) to close at A$3.84 on 1 April, a gain of approximately 19.6% from the low and +15.3% over five trading days, outperforming the ASX200 by nearly 14 percentage points. The move follows a severe sell-off triggered by H1 FY2026 results on 15 March that showed a net loss of US$34.77 million, wider than the prior year, and zero commercial revenue. The critical diagnostic is timing: the stock failed to sustain any recovery for the full week following the earnings crash, then accelerated sharply in the three sessions immediately after three director interest change notices were lodged on 29 March. Until the transaction type in those filings is confirmed as open-market purchases rather than routine equity plan events, the primary catalyst cannot be assigned with confidence. The broad market recovery and deeply oversold technical conditions were enabling factors, not causes. Confidence is low.
report.full_note:
IperionX recovered approximately 19.6% from its 22 March trough of A$3.21 to close at A$3.84 on 1 April, with the five-day return of +15.3% representing outperformance of nearly 14 percentage points relative to the ASX200. The move comes after one of the sharpest single-stock drawdowns on the ASX this year: the stock lost 46% in the three weeks following its 15 March H1 FY2026 earnings release, which showed a net loss of US$34.77 million against US$16.24 million in the prior corresponding period, and confirmed the company remains pre-revenue with zero commercial sales.

The strongest piece of timing evidence is what did not happen. Despite the stock being objectively oversold from 15 March onward, it did not stage a sustained recovery for the full week between 22 and 28 March, re-testing its trough of A$3.21 on 26 March. The recovery then accelerated sharply in three consecutive sessions: A$3.38 on 29 March, A$3.51 on 30 March, and A$3.84 on 31 March, representing a gain of approximately 13% across those three days alone. The 29 March session coincides directly with three Change of Director's Interest Notices lodged on ASX. If those filings represent open-market purchases by insiders acquiring stock at post-earnings lows, they provide the most plausible specific trigger for the inflection. However, these notices are coded as non-market-sensitive and the available data does not confirm whether the underlying transactions were open-market purchases, option exercises, or performance rights vesting. Until that is verified, this ranking must be treated as conditional.

The broader market environment was a secondary contributor, not the cause. The ASX200 recovered roughly 3.7% from its late-March trough over the same window, and the industrials/materials sector ETF gained approximately 2.8%. These tailwinds improved risk appetite for smaller-cap names and likely encouraged re-engagement with speculative positions, but they explain at most 2 to 3 percentage points of IPX's 19.6% recovery. The stock-specific character of the move is clear from the relative outperformance data.

Volume on 1 April was 1.66 million shares, equivalent to approximately 0.49% of the float, which is modest for a stock that had just risen 15% over five sessions. The absence of a volume spike on any single day is more consistent with a gradual short-cover and re-engagement pattern than with a large new institutional buyer or a retail-driven squeeze. The step-up recovery pattern (incremental daily gains rather than a single surge) is also consistent with position covering rather than aggressive new buying.

Retail media coverage from Kalkine and Stocks Down Under referencing the rally and IperionX's strategic assets appears to have lagged the move rather than led it. The Kalkine article titled 'IperionX Stock Soars: Key Reasons Behind the Rally' was published after the recovery was already underway and is best treated as an amplifier of existing momentum among retail participants. No HotCopper, Reddit, or X activity data is available for the period, so social sentiment cannot be assessed directly.

Several explanations were considered and ranked lower. A broker upgrade or analyst note is a material gap in the evidence: the analyst consensus target of A$8.56 implies 123% upside from current levels, and a post-earnings note circulating in the 27 to 30 March window could independently explain the inflection. However, no such note was identified, and assigning it primary status without evidence would be improper. A US critical minerals policy development or defence procurement announcement could also have served as a sector catalyst given IperionX's defence-aligned titanium focus, but no such event was identified in the gathered data. The macro and sector recovery explanation, while confirmed as a contributing factor, is insufficient on its own given the scale of the relative outperformance.

The final judgement is that the most plausible trigger for the sustained recovery from 29 March is the director interest change filings, provided those transactions prove to be open-market purchases. If they are routine equity plan events, the primary driver reverts to a technically enabled oversold bounce without a single identifiable catalyst, which is a less satisfying but still coherent explanation given the scale of the prior drawdown. Confidence is low because the key evidence, being the actual transaction type in the director notices, is unverified. This view would change if the director filings prove to be option exercises rather than purchases, if a broker note or US policy announcement is identified in the 27 to 30 March window, or if short interest data reveals a significant covering dynamic during the recovery period.

evidence_quality.primary_evidence: H1 FY2026 earnings release (15 March) establishing the cause of the drawdown is confirmed via multiple newswire sources. ASX price data for the recovery window is confirmed. Three director interest change notices lodged 29 March are confirmed as filed but transaction type and direction are unverified.
evidence_quality.secondary_evidence: ASX200 and sector ETF index data confirm the macro tailwind. Kalkine and Stocks Down Under articles confirm lagging media coverage. Analyst consensus target of A$8.56 is confirmed via Yahoo Finance data. No broker notes, short interest data, social sentiment data, or peer comparison data are available.
evidence_quality.key_gap: The transaction type in the three director interest change notices dated 29 March is the single most important unresolved question. Whether those transactions are open-market purchases or routine equity plan events determines whether the primary driver is an insider confidence signal or a directionless technical bounce. A secondary gap is the absence of any broker note or US policy announcement in the 27 to 30 March window.

what_would_change_the_view: Confirmation that the 29 March director filings represent option exercises or performance rights vesting rather than open-market purchases would demote this catalyst entirely and revert the primary driver to an unanchored oversold bounce
Identification of a broker note, analyst target price revision, or initiation published in the 27 to 30 March window would likely displace the director filing thesis and become the primary driver

Discovery of a US critical minerals policy announcement, defence procurement award, or DFS milestone disclosure in late March 2026 would rank as a strong stock-specific catalyst not captured in current data

Short interest data showing a significant short position established during the earnings sell-off, followed by covering activity from 29 March, would strengthen the short-cover mechanic as a co-primary driver rather than a secondary one

broker_activity.consensus_change: No broker upgrades, downgrades, or target price changes were identified in the data for the analysis window. Analyst consensus target price is A$8.56 per Yahoo Finance, implying approximately 123% upside from the current A$3.84 price. The absence of broker activity is itself a material evidence gap.

_overcorrection.message: Single-day move of +19.6% exceeds 10% threshold – possible overcorrection
